name: barbell alternate biceps curl
instructions: Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell in each hand, palms facing forward.
Keep your upper arms stationary and exhale as you curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the barbells until your biceps are fully contracted and the barbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale as you slowly begin to lower the barbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, alternating arms.

Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell in each hand, palms facing forward. Keep your upper arms stationary and exhale as you curl the weights while contracting your biceps. Continue to raise the barbells until your biceps are fully contracted and the barbells are at shoulder level. Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps. Inhale as you slowly begin to lower the barbells back to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, alternating arms.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
